User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the iykyk platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the user.

communityIds.description: References to Communities the user belongs to. (Relationship: User N:N Community)

Venue.title: Venue
Venue.description: Represents a venue (e.g., restaurant, bar, fitness studio) in the iykyk platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the venue entity.

address.description: The street address of the venue.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the venue.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the venue.

category.description: The category of the venue (e.g., restaurant, bar).

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.description: Represents an experience or event happening at a venue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the experience entity.

startTime.description: The start time of the experience.

endTime.description: The end time of the experience.

moods.description: An array of moods associated with the experience.

Deal.title: Deal
Deal.description: Represents a deal or promotion offered at a venue or by a creator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the deal entity.

venueName.description: Denormalized name of the venue for efficient querying.

creatorId.description: Reference to User who created this deal. (Relationship: User 1:N Deal)

startTime.description: The start time of the deal.

endTime.description: The end time of the deal.

dealType.description: Used to differentiate between standard 'Deals' and real-time 'Fire' items.

Community.title: Community
Community.description: Represents a themed community within the iykyk platform (e.g., sushi lovers, cocktail hunters).

id.description: Unique identifier for the community entity.

category.description: The category of the community (e.g., food, drink, fitness).

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership for user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores venue information. Top-level entity for easy querying.

params.name: venueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the venue.

definition.description: Stores experiences associated with a venue. Includes denormalized 'venueId' for authorization independence.

params.name: venueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the venue.

params.name: experienceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the experience.

definition.description: A top-level collection for all deals, fire items, and perks. This allows for efficient global queries, like finding all active 'fire' deals, without needing to query every venue subcollection. Denormalized data like venueName is included to support this.

params.name: dealId
params.description: The unique identifier of the deal.

definition.description: Stores community information. Includes a 'members' map (uid: role) for role-based access control.

params.name: communityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the community.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support iykyk's real-time cultural portal concept. It prioritizes efficient querying for features like the 'Fire' feed by using a top-level denormalized '/deals' collection. This avoids slow, expensive 'fan-out' queries that would be required if deals were nested under each venue. Other entities like users, venues, and communities are structured for clear access patterns and scalable security rules.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**

*   Authorization is implemented using path-based ownership for private user data and membership maps for collaborative data. Where access to a subcollection depends on attributes of a parent collection, these attributes are denormalized into the subcollection documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, promoting atomic operations and simplifying debugging.

**QAPs (Queryability and Security):**

*   **Segregation:** Data with different security requirements is stored in separate collections. For example, user-owned data resides under `/users/{userId}`, while venue and community data are stored in top-level collections with appropriate security rules.
*   **Membership Maps:** Communities utilize a `members` map to manage user roles, enabling secure listing of community members and controlling access to community-specific data.

**Explanation of Collections:**

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles, providing a dedicated path for user-specific data and simplifying ownership-based security rules.
*   `/venues/{venueId}`: Stores venue information as top-level entities for easy querying.
*   `/venues/{venueId}/experiences/{experienceId}`: Stores experiences associated with a venue. The `venueId` is denormalized for authorization independence.
*   `/deals/{dealId}`: A denormalized, top-level collection for all deals and 'Fire' items. This is crucial for the performance of the 'iykyk Fire' feed, allowing a single query to fetch all relevant, time-sensitive items across all venues.
*   `/communities/{communityId}`: Stores community information, with a `members` map for role-based access control.
